def solve(nums : Array(Int64), maxC : Int64) : Int64
  n = nums.size
  return 0_i64 if maxC >= n

  low = 1_i64
  high = n.to_i64
  answer = n.to_i64

  while low <= high
    mid = (low + high) // 2
    if is_possible(nums, maxC, mid)
      answer = mid
      high = mid - 1
    else
      low = mid + 1
    end
  end

  has_stable_element = nums.any? { |x| x >= 2 }
  return 0_i64 unless has_stable_element
  answer
end

private def is_possible(nums : Array(Int64), maxC : Int64, stability : Int64) : Bool
  n = nums.size
  return true if stability >= n

  required_changes = 0_i64
  left = 0_i64
  right = 0_i64

  while right < n
    if right - left + 1 > stability
      current_gcd = compute_gcd_of_subarray(nums, left, right)
      if current_gcd >= 2
        required_changes += 1
        left = right
        return false if required_changes > maxC
      else
        left += 1
      end
    end
    right += 1
  end

  required_changes <= maxC
end

private def compute_gcd_of_subarray(nums : Array(Int64), left : Int64, right : Int64) : Int64
  current_gcd = nums[left]
  (left + 1..right).each do |i|
    current_gcd = current_gcd.gcd(nums[i])
    break if current_gcd < 2
  end
  current_gcd
end

    # Binary search to find the minimal stability
    low = 1
    high = n
    answer = n
    while low <= high:
        mid = (low + high) // 2
        if is_possible(mid):
            answer = mid
            high = mid - 1
        else:
            low = mid + 1
    
    # Also need to check if answer can be 0 (no stable subarrays at all)
    # If maxC is sufficient to break all elements (i.e., set all to 1 or 0)
    if maxC >= n:
        return 0
    
    # Check if the minimal stability is 1 (since single elements can be stable if >=2)
    # So the minimal possible is at least 1 unless all elements are modified to <2
    has_stable_element = any(x >= 2 for x in nums)
    if not has_stable_element:
        return 0
    if answer == 1:
        return 1
    
    # Otherwise, need to see if we can break all subarrays of length >= answer
    # So the minimal stability is the minimal answer where is_possible(answer) is True
    return answer if answer != n else 0
